passages.title: Save energy, save our Earth!
paragraphs: Mother Earth is running out of resources! What should we do? Here are our top three ideas. We should all try to save energy and save our Earth!
Useful tips to save water: Take a quick shower. Wash all your dirty clothes together, not one at a time. Wash fruit and vegetables, then use the water again.
Useful tips to use less electricity: Use LED lighting. Turn off your TV or computer and unplug it. Set your air conditioner to 26℃ or more in summer.
Great tips to save and recycle resources: Use both sides of paper. Use old bottles again. You can put noodles or pencils in them.

passages.title: Letter from the Editor
paragraphs: How can we all help Mother Earth? We are running a competition to find some helpful ideas from our readers. Please write to us and let us know your ideas and tips. Thank you.

passages.title: Making a solar oven
paragraphs: It was a sunny day. Grandpa, Binbin and Robin played outside.
They didn't want to go back to the kitchen to cook lunch. So, what could they do?
Grandpa had an idea. "The sun is very hot. We can use solar power to cook our lunch here," he said. "Let's make a solar oven!" So, they did.
"Wow! How does the box keep the heat? Why do we use black paper?" Binbin wanted to know.
"The food is delicious. And we cooked with no gas or electricity! That's super cool!" said Binbin.
"Sunlight is a great source of energy. It can help light our house. It can run cars. And we can use it to cook too!" said Robin.
"Yes, we should use energy in smart ways," said Grandpa with a smile.
